Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The main steps are Claim Registration CR Document Verification DV Fraud Check FC Claim Assessment CA Approval AP Payment P Notification N Closure CL But there are loops if documents are incomplete after DV go to RDS Re Document Submission then back to DV Similarly if FC raises doubts do II In depth Investigation which can loop back to FC multiple times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the basic flow is probably something like Start to CR to DV to if incomplete RDS to DV to FC to if fraud doubts II to FC to CA to if over threshold AP to P to N to CL to End

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait but the problem says after DV if incomplete RDS happens before continuing So DV leads to either FC or RDS Similarly FC leads to either CA or II

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: in Petri nets places are states transitions are activities So each activity transition consumes tokens from input places and produces tokens in output places

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Start place lets call it start has a token initially CR transition takes token from start produces token in a place after CR maybe after CR Then from after CR the next step is DV So DV transition consumes token from after CR and produces token in after DV

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: after DV there are two possibilities if documents are okay proceed to FC if not go to RDS So after DV place would have two outgoing arcs one to FC transition and one to RDS transition

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: So the places would be start to CR to place1 after CR place1 to DV to place2 after DV place2 has two transitions FC and RDS FC transition leads to place3 after FC RDS transition leads to place4 after RDS place4 leads back to place1 so that after RDS you do DV again

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait no Wait after DV is place2 If you go to RDS then after RDS you need to do DV again So RDS transition should lead to a place that connects back to DV

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Wait maybe a better way is to have a place ready for dv that is fed by both CR and RDS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: ready for dv place is the input to DV transition CR transition produces to ready for dv DV transition consumes from ready for dv produces to dv done dv done place has arcs to FC and RDS transitions RDS transition consumes from dv done produces to ready for dv

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Similarly for FC and II ready for fc place is input to FC transition FC transition consumes from ready for fc produces to fc done fc done place has arcs to CA and II transitions II transition consumes from fc done produces to ready for fc

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After CA depending on amount either go to AP or directly to P So CA transition leads to a place that has two outgoing arcs one to AP transition and one to P transition AP transition leads to place ap done which feeds into P

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So p ready is fed by both the direct path ca done to p ready and the AP path ap done to p ready Then P transition consumes from p ready and produces to p done

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Initial marking token in start Final marking token in cl done

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Ah right This is a common mistake when modeling OR conditions in Petri nets To model an OR condition either path you need to use a place that collects tokens from both paths but the transition should have only one input place

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The correct way is to have a single input place for the transition which is fed by multiple places For example for the DV transition the input place is ready for dv which is fed by cr done and rds done So cr done has an arc to ready for dv rds done has an arc to ready for dv DV transition consumes from ready for dv

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Similarly fc done has two outgoing arcs to CA transitions input place ready for ca and to II transition II transitions output is ii done which feeds into ready for fc

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This model should work

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Places start cr done ready for dv dv done rds done ready for fc fc done ii done ready for ca ca done ap ready ap done p ready p done n ready n done cl ready cl done Transitions CR DV RDS FC II CA AP P N CL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Arcs between places and transitions cr done to ready for dv rds done to ready for dv dv done to ready for fc and dv done to RDS fc done to ready for ca and fc done to II ca done to ap ready and ca done to p ready ap done to p ready p done to n ready n done to cl ready

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The process ends with CL transition producing to cl done So final marking is cl done with one token

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now lets write the Python code using pm4py We need to create a PetriNet add places and transitions add arcs then define initial and final markings
